## Input Files

- `www/assets/sprites/enemies/suppressor/suppressor_alien_mk1_master.png`
- `www/assets/sprites/enemies/suppressor/suppressor_alien_elite.png`

- `www/assets/sprites/enemies/suppressor/suppressor_alien_brute.png`

## Faction Visual Language

Suppressor Alien units use broad armored bodies, thick biomechanical shell plating, red-orange alien cores, and industrial-organic siege structures. They are intentionally heavier, warmer, and more oppressive than the Scout Alien faction.

## Silhouette Validation

The faction reads as a heavy alien threat. Mk1 is the baseline pressure unit, elite is denser and more command-like, artillery has an elongated bombardment form, and brute is the widest tank-like silhouette. All variants maintain the same heavy shell and red-orange energy identity.

## Readability Findings

The sprites remain readable on black and dark blue backgrounds, inside dense bullet clutter, and in mixed pressure formations. Large armor panels and warm energy cores provide clear recognition at 64x64 while keeping internal detail controlled.

## Android Readability Findings

The recommended gameplay size is 64x64 or larger. Red-orange energy markers and broad shell silhouettes remain visible on mobile-scale displays. Brute and elite should be spaced carefully in dense groups because their wide forms can merge if overlapped too tightly.

## Remaining Weaknesses

The faction intentionally uses wide silhouettes, so formation spacing matters more than it does for agile Scout units. Artillery is the tallest/longest read and benefits from being used where its frontal direction is visible.

## Recommended Gameplay Usage

Use mk1 as the standard frontline pressure unit, elite as a tougher command-style enemy, artillery as a ranged bombardment threat, and brute as a wide tank-like blocker. Runtime hitboxes, AI behavior, balance, and rank should remain independent from these visual assets.
